players.name: Yahia Fofana
players.bio: Young shot-stopper who earned his stripes in Turkey with Çaykur Rizespor. At just 25, Fofana brings agility and composure to the Elephants' last line of defence, making this World Cup a landmark moment in his blossoming career.
players.facts: Born in 2000, Fofana is part of a golden generation — he shares his birth year with several of Côte d'Ivoire's rising stars.

players.name: Ousmane Diomande
players.bio: A commanding centre-back who rose rapidly through Sporting CP to become one of Europe's most sought-after young defenders. Born in 2003, Diomandé brings physicality and composure beyond his years to Les Éléphants' backline.
players.facts: Ousmane Diomandé was born in Guinea-Bissau and moved to Côte d'Ivoire as a child before earning his international call-up.

players.name: Ghislain Konan
players.bio: A dependable left-back with over 50 caps to his name, Konan has been a cornerstone of the Ivorian defence for years. Now plying his trade in Portugal with Gil Vicente, the veteran brings experience and reliability to the squad.
players.facts: Konan spent several seasons at Reims in Ligue 1, becoming one of the most consistent full-backs in French football.

players.name: Jean Michaël Seri
players.bio: A cultured central midfielder with 65 caps, Séri has graced clubs like Nice, Fulham, and Barcelona before landing at Maribor. His vision and passing range have made him a reliable engine in Côte d'Ivoire's midfield for over a decade.
players.facts: Séri came agonisingly close to joining Barcelona in 2017 — the deal famously collapsed over a €40m release clause dispute.

players.name: Wilfried Singo
players.bio: Born on Christmas Day 2000, Singo is quite the gift — a powerful, versatile defender equally effective at right-back or centre-back. At Galatasaray he's developed into a menacing presence and a key figure in this Ivorian squad.
players.facts: Singo is literally a Christmas baby — born 25 December 2000 in Grand-Béréby. His parents clearly delivered early.

players.name: Seko Fofana
players.bio: Born in Paris but proudly Ivorian, Seko Fofana is a box-to-box powerhouse who dominated Ligue 1 with Lens before moving to Porto. With 7 international goals, his engine and elegance make him one of Africa's finest midfielders.
players.facts: Fofana captained RC Lens to a Champions League return — a feat that made him a hero in northern France's mining heartland.

players.name: Odilon Kossounou
players.bio: An imposing centre-back who won the UEFA Europa League with Atalanta, Kossounou combines explosive pace with defensive intelligence. At just 25, the Abidjan-born defender is already one of West Africa's most complete defenders.
players.facts: Kossounou played for Atalanta's historic 2023–24 Europa League-winning side — the club's first-ever major European trophy.

players.name: Franck Kessié
players.bio: The Président. With over 100 caps, Kessiéis the heartbeat of Ivorian football — a relentless, commanding midfielder who starred at Atalanta, AC Milan, and Barcelona. Now in Saudi Arabia, his leadership at 29 remains absolutely priceless.
players.facts: Kessié won back-to-back Serie A titles with AC Milan (2022) and was nicknamed 'Il Presidente' by the Rossoneri faithful.

players.name: Ange-Yoan Bonny
players.bio: A highly-rated young striker who made the remarkable leap to Inter Milan, Bonny is a clinical finisher with sharp movement. Born in France, he's chosen to represent Les Éléphants and arrives at his first World Cup with enormous potential.
players.facts: Bonny joins the illustrious ranks of players to wear Inter Milan's famous black and blue — at just 22, no pressure at all.

players.name: Simon Adingra
players.bio: A key player in Côte d'Ivoire's 2023 AFCON triumph on home soil, Adingra dazzles with pace, dribbling, and an eye for goal. The Monaco winger began New Year's Day 2002 with a bang — literally, that's his birthday.
players.facts: Adingra was born on 1 January 2002 — he's the ideal New Year's resolution: fast, exciting, and full of promise.

players.name: Yan Diomande
players.bio: Barely 19 and already racking up goals for RB Leipzig, Yan Diomandé is one of the most exciting teenagers in European football. This World Cup could be the launchpad for what promises to be a stellar career.
players.facts: Born in 2006, Yan Diomandé could become one of the youngest Ivorians ever to play at a FIFA World Cup.

players.name: Elye Wahi
players.bio: A pacey, technically gifted striker who cut his teeth at Montpellier and moved to Nice, Wahi is still finding his international feet with just 2 caps. Born in France, he's chosen the Elephants and will be hungry to prove himself on the biggest stage.
players.facts: Wahi scored 19 Ligue 1 goals in a single season at Montpellier aged just 19 — not bad for a teenager from Courcouronnes.

players.name: Christopher Opéri
players.bio: A composed and attack-minded left-back, Opéri has built his career across Europe, landing at İstanbul Başakşehir. With 12 caps, the Abidjan native is a reliable option in the Ivorian backline and brings Turkish league experience.
players.facts: Opéri has played club football in Belgium, France, and Turkey — quite the passport collection for a left-back.

players.name: Oumar Diakité
players.bio: A lively, goalscoring forward who has impressed at Cercle Brugge in Belgium, Diakité brings energy and a nose for goal. Born in Bingerville in 2003, he's one of the more exciting young forwards in the Ivorian setup.
players.facts: Diakité plays for Cercle Brugge — one of Belgium's oldest clubs, founded in 1899 and backed by AS Monaco's ownership group.

players.name: Amad Diallo
players.bio: The darling of Old Trafford, Amad Diallo has dazzled Manchester United fans with his flair and goals. The Abidjan-born winger chose Côte d'Ivoire over France and brings 6 international goals and bags of confidence to this squad.
players.facts: Amad scored a last-minute derby winner against Manchester City in the FA Cup in 2024 — instant United legend status.

players.name: Mohamed Koné
players.bio: Uncapped at international level heading into 2026, Mohamed Koné earned his World Cup call-up through consistent performances at Charleroi in Belgium. The young goalkeeper from Adjamé is ready to learn from his experienced squad-mates.
players.facts: Koné is yet to earn a senior cap — making him one of very few players to potentially make their international debut at a World Cup.

players.name: Guéla Doué
players.bio: A energetic right-back with an attacking instinct, Douéhas registered 3 international goals from defence — a remarkable return. Born in Angers, he's the younger brother of Crystal Palace winger Désiré Dallé and plays with the same flair.
players.facts: Guéla Douéis the elder brother of Chelsea's Désiré Doué — the Douéfamily is quietly taking over European football.

players.name: Ibrahim Sangaré
players.bio: A powerhouse defensive midfielder who has become a fan favourite at Nottingham Forest, Sangaré combines physicality with remarkable range of passing. His 12 international goals from midfield defy his defensive billing entirely.
players.facts: Sangaré is so dominant in midfield that Forest fans nicknamed him 'The Wall' — 12 international goals suggest the wall attacks too.

players.name: Nicolas Pépé
players.bio: Once the world's most expensive African player when Arsenal paid £72m for him in 2019, Pépé's career has taken twists and turns. Now at Villarreal, the tricky winger still possesses the electric pace and skill that made him so coveted.
players.kit: Nike Mercurial Vapor XIII Elite FG
players.facts: Pépé's £72m Arsenal fee in 2019 made him the most expensive African player in history at the time — a record that defined an era.

players.name: Emmanuel Agbadou
players.bio: A tall, commanding centre-back who has carved out a solid career in Turkey with Beşiktaş, Agbadou offers aerial dominance and positional awareness. Born in Abidjan, his 20 caps show he's a trusted figure in the Ivorian defensive set-up.
players.facts: Agbadou previously played in Belgium with Genk before his move to Turkey — fluent in the art of defending in multiple leagues.

players.name: Evan Ndicka
players.bio: A stylish, left-footed centre-back born in Paris, Ndicka chose Côte d'Ivoire over France and has never looked back. At Roma, he's developed into a composed, ball-playing defender who brings a touch of elegance to the backline.
players.facts: Ndicka suffered a cardiac scare during a Serie A match in 2024 but made a full recovery — an incredibly courageous comeback story.

players.name: Evann Guessand
players.bio: A versatile, mobile forward born in Ajaccio, Corsica, Guessand brings directness and creativity to the Ivorian attack. With 4 goals in 21 caps, he's established himself as a useful option for Les Éléphants across multiple attacking positions.
players.facts: Guessand was born in Ajaccio — Napoleon Bonaparte's birthplace. Whether he has imperial ambitions on the pitch remains to be seen.

players.name: Alban Lafont
players.bio: Born in Ouagadougou, Burkina Faso, Lafont grew up to become a technically excellent goalkeeper with experience at Toulouse, Nantes, and now Panathinaikos. Eligible for Côte d'Ivoire through heritage, he provides solid backup between the sticks.
players.facts: Lafont was born in Burkina Faso but represents Côte d'Ivoire — and made his Toulouse debut aged just 16, one of France's youngest ever keepers.

players.name: Bazoumana Touré
players.bio: One of the youngest members of the squad, Touré arrived at Hoffenheim as a teenage sensation from Bouaké. The left winger has already scored twice in six caps, showcasing the blistering pace and directness that has clubs across Europe watching closely.
players.facts: Born in March 2006, Touré is one of the youngest players in the entire tournament — he could be a World Cup wildcard sensation.

players.name: Parfait Guiagon
players.bio: Hailing from the small town of Logoualé, Guiagon's journey to a World Cup squad is one of football's feel-good stories. The left winger at Charleroi works hard, runs channels tirelessly, and brings hunger that only a long road to the top can forge.
players.facts: Guiagon hails from Logoualé — a small town in western Côte d'Ivoire. Proof that football talent grows everywhere, not just big cities.

players.name: Christ Inao Oulaï
players.bio: A teenage central midfielder from Yopougon, Oulaï is one of the most exciting young prospects in Ivorian football. At Trabzonspor he's gaining invaluable top-flight experience, and at just 20 he arrives at this World Cup with his whole future ahead.
players.facts: Born in April 2006, Oulaï could be playing in a World Cup before he's old enough to legally drink in many of the host countries.
